parameters.content:
## Generate Blog Drafts from Content Briefs with Tally, OpenAI and Google Docs

### How it works
1. A marketer submits a content brief through a Tally form: topic, target audience, tone, keywords, CTA, and optional competitor URLs.
2. The Tally Trigger fires on submission. Agent 1 parses the raw Tally payload into clean, structured brief fields.
3. Agent 2 builds a detailed blog post outline with section headings and bullet points. If competitor URLs were provided in the Tally form, it factors their angles in.
4. Agent 3 expands the outline into a complete blog post draft written in the specified tone.
5. The finished draft is saved as a new Google Doc, ready for human review.

### Setup
1. Create your content brief form in Tally with fields: Blog Topic, Target Audience, Tone (dropdown), Keywords, CTA, Competitor URLs (optional).
2. Connect the Tally Trigger node and select your Tally form from the dropdown.
3. Add your OpenAI API key under Credentials > OpenAI.
4. Add Google Docs OAuth2 credentials.
5. Submit a test brief through your Tally form. A new Google Doc will appear in the root of your Drive.

### Customization
- Edit Agent 3's system prompt to enforce your brand voice or word count target.
- Tally's conditional logic lets you show/hide the competitor URLs field based on a checkbox.

## Data Capture & Parsing

Tally Trigger captures the content brief. Agent 1 normalizes the raw form data into structured fields. The Code node cleans the AI output for downstream processing.

## Outline & Draft Generation

Agent 2 builds a structured blog outline with section headings and bullet points. Agent 3 expands it into a complete draft in the requested tone.

## Google Docs Output

Prepare Doc Content formats the draft. Create Google Doc saves it as a new document. Update Google Doc inserts the body text.
